In huggingface/transformers at the given commit, explain how the rotary position embedding (RoPE) is applied to query and key tensors during attention computation. Include: (1) the file and function where the rotation is performed, (2) the shape transformations applied, and (3) which model architectures call this helper.

judge.title: Identifies the helper function (e.g. apply_rotary_pos_emb) and the file it lives in (e.g. modeling_llama.py or a shared rope utility module).
Explains that the rotation is applied to query and key tensors (not values) by interleaving or pairing dimensions and rotating with cos/sin embeddings.
Lists at least two transformer architectures that call the helper (e.g. Llama, Mistral, Qwen2, Falcon).
Mentions the head dimension layout (e.g. last-dim split into two halves) used by the rotation.

In kubernetes/kubernetes at the given commit, describe the lifecycle of a Pod from kubelet's PodWorker perspective. In particular: (1) where the per-pod work goroutine is created, (2) what triggers a sync vs a kill update, and (3) how the worker decides a pod is finished.

judge.title: Points at pkg/kubelet/pod_workers.go and the managePodLoop / podWorkerLoop function as the per-pod goroutine.
Explains that UpdatePod with kill==true (or a graceful kill update) drives termination, and a regular sync update drives the reconcile path.
Identifies the finished/terminated state transitions (e.g. SyncTerminatedPod or completeWork removing the worker entry once the pod is fully terminated).
Mentions that the worker channel buffers pending updates so kubelet does not block on slow per-pod sync.
